canto_title: The Prophecies of the Two Celestial Lights

scenes.summary: Dante reflects on the explanation given to him by Carlo regarding the deceits of the descendants of Clemenza.
A moralizing outburst directed at souls who turn away from divine goodness toward earthly vanity.
A new celestial splendor approaches Dante, with Beatrice's eyes fixed upon him.
Dante prays for the spirit to act upon his will and clarify his thoughts.
The first spirit describes its origin as a flame rising from a hill in the Italian countryside.
The spirit foretells the changing course of rivers and the dominance of a powerful lord in the Po valley.
The spirit warns of a corrupt pastor and the heavy toll of bloodshed in Ferrara.
The spirit falls silent and returns to its previous circular motion.
A second, brilliant light appears like a sun-struck arrow, though its shadow brings sadness.
Dante addresses the spirit, asking why its song does not satisfy his desires.

scenes.scene_name: The Geography of the Great Valley
scenes.summary: The second spirit describes the vast valley stretching between the Ebro and the Macra rivers.
The spirit reveals its name as Folco and explains how its presence reflects the divine order.
The spirit identifies itself as Raab and recounts its role in the victory of Joshua.
The spirit critiques the corruption of the Papacy and foretells the eventual liberation of Rome.
